example: The pandemic caused unprecedented disruption to the global economy.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + precedent(선례) + ed = 선례가 없는 → 전례 없는. pre(앞)+ced(가다): 앞서 간 적 없는

example: The manager connived at the employees' misuse of company funds.
mnemonic: con(함께) + nive(눈짓하다, 라틴어 nivere) = 함께 눈짓하며 → 묵인하다, 공모하다

example: The company's stock price reached its nadir during the financial crisis.
mnemonic: 아랍어 nazir as-samt(반대편 지점). 천정(zenith)의 정반대 → 최저점

example: Fashion trends are fickle and change with every season.
mnemonic: '피클' → '피클(pickle)' 시큼했다 달았다 변덕스러운!

example: The reformers sought to expose the iniquities of the corrupt regime.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + iqu(평등한, 라틴어 aequus) + ity = 평등하지 않음 → 부정, 사악함. equ(같은): equal, equity, equitable

example: She gave him an incredulous look when he claimed to have met the president.
mnemonic: '인크레줄러스' → 'in(아닌)+credulous(잘 믿는)' 잘 믿지 않는!

example: The documentary was designed to edify viewers about environmental issues.
mnemonic: ed(세우다, 라틴어 aedis=건물) + ify = 정신의 건물을 세우다 → 교화하다. edif(건물): edifice(건물, 대건축)

example: His gauche behavior at the formal dinner amused the other guests.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 gauche(왼쪽의). 왼손잡이가 서투르다는 관념에서 → 서투른. 영어 left도 비슷한 어원

example: The judge sought to reach an equitable settlement for both parties.
mnemonic: equit(공정한, 라틴어 aequitas) + able = 공정할 수 있는. equ(같은): equal, equity, equanimity, adequate

example: Socrates was charged with impiety for questioning the gods of Athens.
mnemonic: im(아닌) + piety(경건, 라틴어 pietas) = 경건하지 않은 → 불경. pi(경건): pious, piety, expiate

example: The daughter delivered a moving eulogy at her father's funeral.
mnemonic: eu(좋은, 그리스어) + logy(말) = 좋은 말 → 추도사, 찬사. eu(좋은): euphoria, euphemism, euthanasia

example: The committee declared the outdated regulation otiose and recommended its removal.
mnemonic: oti(한가한, 라틴어 otium=여가) + ose = 한가한 → 쓸모없는. otium(여가): negotiate(neg+otium=여가가 아닌=바쁜 일)

example: She resisted the blandishments of the advertising industry.
mnemonic: bland(부드러운) + ish + ment = 부드러운 말로 꾀는 것 → 아첨. bland(부드러운): 부드럽게 달래는

example: The overwrought mother panicked when her child was five minutes late.
mnemonic: over(지나치게) + wrought(worked, 만들어진) = 지나치게 만들어진 → 과도하게 흥분한

example: Ancient peoples offered sacrifices to propitiate angry gods.
mnemonic: pro(앞에) + piti(호의적인, 라틴어 propitius) + ate = 호의적으로 만들다 → 달래다. propitious(유리한)와 같은 어근

example: After the shipwreck, flotsam washed up on the shore for weeks.
mnemonic: float(뜨다)와 관련, 고대 프랑스어 floter(떠다니다). 바다에 떠다니는 잔해 → 표류물

example: The restaurant offers a unique gustatory experience with its fusion cuisine.
mnemonic: gust(맛, 라틴어 gustare=맛보다) + atory = 맛에 관한 → 미각의. gust(맛): gusto, disgust

example: He dissimulated his true feelings behind a mask of indifference.
mnemonic: dis(부정) + simul(같은, 라틴어 simulare=흉내내다) + ate = 같지 않게 하다 → 숨기다. simul(같은): simulate, similar, simultaneous

example: The satirical show lampooned politicians from all parties.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 lampon(마시자!, 풍자 노래의 후렴구). 술 마시며 부르는 풍자 노래 → 풍자하다

example: The opposition's strident criticism of the policy dominated the news cycle.
mnemonic: strid(삐걱거리다, 라틴어 stridere) + ent = 삐걱거리는 → 귀에 거슬리는, 강경한

example: The voluble guide kept up a constant stream of commentary throughout the tour.
mnemonic: volu(구르다, 라틴어 volvere) + ble = 말이 술술 굴러나오는 → 유창한. volv(구르다): revolve, evolve, involve

example: The scandal embroiled several high-ranking government officials.
mnemonic: '임브로일' → 'em(안에)+broil(끓이다)' 분쟁 속에 끓이듯 휘말리게!

example: The wheat fields undulated gently in the summer breeze.
mnemonic: und(물결, 라틴어 unda) + ulate = 물결치다 → 파도치다. und(물결): inundate, abundant, redundant

example: The company announced mass redundancies to cut operating costs.
mnemonic: re(다시) + und(넘치다, 라틴어 undare) + ancy = 다시 넘치는 → 잉여, 중복. redundant(잉여의)의 명사형

example: The documentary illuminated the hidden complexities of the justice system.
mnemonic: il(안에) + lumin(빛, 라틴어 lumen) + ate = 빛을 비추다 → 밝히다. lum(빛): luminous, lucid, translucent

example: His hubris led him to underestimate his opponents and lose the match.
mnemonic: '휴브리스' → '휴(huge)+bris' 거대한 자만심 = 오만!

example: The DNA evidence provided irrefutable proof of the suspect's guilt.
mnemonic: ir(아닌) + refut(반박하다) + able = 반박할 수 없는. refute(반박하다)

example: The ornate ceiling of the cathedral took decades to complete.
mnemonic: orn(장식하다, 라틴어 ornare) + ate = 장식된 → 화려한. orn(장식): ornament, adorn

example: She often looked back on her halcyon days of childhood with nostalgia.
mnemonic: 그리스 신화 alkyon(물총새). 물총새가 바다를 잠재우는 평화로운 날 → 평화로운, 행복한

example: The committee voted to censure the member for ethical violations.
mnemonic: cens(판단하다, 라틴어 censere) + ure = 판단하여 비난하다. cens(판단): censor(검열), census(인구조사=평가)

example: I appreciate your candor in telling me the truth about the situation.
mnemonic: cand(빛나다, 하얀, 라틴어 candidus) + or = 하얗게 빛나는 것 → 솔직함. cand(빛나다): candid, candle, candidate

example: The meeting descended into mutual recrimination rather than productive discussion.
mnemonic: re(되돌려) + crimin(비난, 고발) + ation = 비난을 되돌리다 → 맞비난. crimin(고발): crime, criminal, incriminate

example: The film was criticized for its gratuitous violence.
mnemonic: '그러튜이터스' → 'gratu(무료)+itous' 주지 않아도 될 불필요한!

example: His insouciant attitude toward deadlines frustrated his coworkers.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + souci(걱정, 프랑스어) + ant = 걱정하지 않는 → 태평한. souci(걱정): 라틴어 sollicitus(걱정하는)에서 유래

example: The phlegmatic commander remained calm even under heavy enemy fire.
mnemonic: phlegm(점액, 그리스어 phlegma) + atic = 점액질의 → 침착한. 고대 체액설: 점액=냉정한 기질

example: The bakery offers a delectable selection of pastries and cakes.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + lect(기쁘게 하다, 라틴어 delectare) + able = 완전히 기쁘게 하는 → 맛있는. lect(기쁨): delight, delicious

example: The celebrity did not deign to speak with the local reporters.
mnemonic: 라틴어 dignare(가치 있다고 여기다), dignus(가치 있는). 가치를 낮추어 → 굽어하다. dign(가치): dignity, disdain

example: I cannot fathom why anyone would choose to live in such a dangerous area.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 faethm(양팔 벌린 길이, 측정 단위). 깊이를 재다 → 이해하다, 헤아리다. 1 fathom = 약 1.8m(해양 깊이 단위)

example: The government arrested the journalist for publishing seditious material.
mnemonic: sed(떨어져) + it(가다) + ious = 떨어져 나가려는 → 반역의. sed(떨어져)+it(가다): 정부에서 떨어져 가는

example: The dark clouds presaged a violent storm approaching from the west.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + sage(느끼다, 라틴어 sagire) = 미리 느끼다 → 전조가 되다. sage(현자)와 같은 어원

example: The term was originally neutral but has acquired a pejorative meaning over time.
mnemonic: pejor(더 나쁜, 라틴어 peior) + ative = 더 나쁘게 만드는 → 경멸적인. pejor(나쁜): impair

example: The craven soldier fled the battlefield at the first sign of danger.
mnemonic: 고대 프랑스어 cravante(패배한, 굴복한)에서 유래. 굴복한 → 비겁한

example: The drill sergeant's stentorian voice could be heard across the entire field.
mnemonic: Stentor(스텐토르, 트로이 전쟁에서 50명 목소리를 낸 그리스 전사) + ian → 우렁찬

example: The document was stored in a hermetic container to prevent damage.
mnemonic: '허메틱' → 'Hermes(헤르메스)+tic' 연금술사 헤르메스의 밀봉 기술!

example: The petulant child threw a tantrum when denied a new toy.
mnemonic: pet(공격하다, 라틴어 petere) + ulant = 공격적으로 투정하는 → 짜증 잘 내는

example: The rebels faced an implacable enemy that showed no mercy.
mnemonic: '임플래커블' → 'im(불)+placable(달랠 수 있는)' 달랠 수 없는 무자비한!

example: The court ordered the arrest record to be expunged after the acquittal.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + punge(찌르다, 라틴어 pungere) = 찔러서 밖으로 빼내다 → 삭제하다. pung(찌르다): puncture, pungent, expunge

example: The programmer concatenated the two data sets into one file.
mnemonic: con(함께) + caten(사슬, 라틴어 catena) + ate = 사슬로 함께 잇다 → 연결하다. caten(사슬): chain, concatenation

example: The art dealer verified the provenance of the painting before the auction.
mnemonic: pro(앞에서) + ven(오다, 라틴어 venire) + ance = 어디에서 왔는가 → 출처. ven(오다): advent, convention, prevent

example: The sudden resignation of the CEO caused great perturbation in the markets.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + turb(어지럽히다, 라틴어 turbare) + ation = 완전히 어지럽힘 → 동요. turb(어지럽히다): disturb, turbulent, turbid
